reviewer: AI-assisted visual inspection against rendered source PDF pages
sampling: All five pages below 0.80 token recall plus the only source page that produced chunks above 512 tokens before repair.

checks.reason: lowest page-level token recall; two dense mathematical tables
checks.finding: Table structure, labels, attribute indices, and source-page provenance are retained. Formula tokenization and repeated spanning cells explain most overlap loss; dense symbolic table content should still be cited from the PDF.

checks.reason: second-lowest token recall; two-column appendix dominated by equations
checks.finding: The rendered page is equation-dense. Formula records, LaTeX overlays, page references, and source crops preserve the content more faithfully than plain token overlap measures.

checks.reason: low text recall on a page containing only two precision-recall plots
checks.finding: Both plot images and their captions are preserved. Curve pixels are not converted into numeric series, so text-only retrieval cannot recover exact plotted values.

checks.reason: low token recall on a proof and equation-heavy page
checks.finding: Definitions, proposition text, formula crops, and reconstructed LaTeX are present with page provenance; symbol-level PDF text extraction accounts for the lower lexical overlap.

checks.reason: low token recall on formulas and a compact feature table
checks.finding: Z-score, min-max, decimal-precision, label formulas, and the feature table are present. Formula overlays and table-cell serialization introduce spacing differences but preserve the values.

checks.reason: only page with chunks above the 512-token hard input boundary before repair
checks.finding: All Table III model names and reported metrics match the rendered PDF. The two oversized table chunks were deterministically split into four chunks below the hard limit without dropping content.
